threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You think the 'night tube' is a thing? Try catching a bus after midnight—good luck with that. Welcome to Hackney.
Last night on the 159, a guy in a full tuxedo was belting out opera. Everyone was too stunned to even clap.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a group of kids skateboarding on the disused railway tracks, dodging pigeons and laughter echoing—this place really felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you see street art on bus stops turned into mini-galleries, showcasing local artists right next to the traffic chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name the taco truck within a block of any given mural. Outsiders just photograph the art.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni the other day, and a guy started reciting Shakespeare to a confused dog. Only in the Tenderloin does poetry ride the rails.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is all about its plazas, but it’s the hidden alleys filled with murals and local cafés that breathe real life into the neighborhood.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look baffled when the bus driver yells "¡A la siguiente!" and instinctively check their phones for the next stop.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 'Bus Stop' signs mean you can catch a tube in a hurry. Good luck on strike days!
Caught a whiff of jollof rice on the 35 last night, turns out the guy sitting across was just carrying takeout from Mama's. Smelled amazing!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw the random karaoke night in the pub while the kebab shop owner waved at the guy juggling outside. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find a bar that serves beer brewed from leftover bread. Only in Berlin would we toast to sustainability with a hoppy twist.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s really local if they can name the spot where the taco truck used to park before the city trucked it away last year.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The other day, a guy serenaded us with a ukulele on the 5. Only in SF do you get delayed by artistry and a dog on the tracks.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but they miss out on the little plazas where locals gather, sipping vermut and chatting until late.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re always staring at their phone for directions while standing directly in front of a metro map. Classic giveaway.
